text: Implementing strong security measures is a fundamental aspect of modern web development. First, ensure your site uses HTTPS to encrypt data between the server and users, protecting sensitive information like passwords. Next, always validate and sanitize any input from users before processing it; this prevents common attacks such as SQL injection or cross-site scripting (XSS). Additionally, use robust authentication systems with password hashing and consider adding multi-factor authentication for an extra layer of protection. Finally, be aware of common vulnerabilities like broken access controls or insecure direct object references. Regularly testing and updating your code helps safeguard user data and maintain trust.
